_note: Canonical 3-layer PPO glossary for tooltips across the hub, carrier hubs, and spokes. Mirrors the live page's depth: def = plain definition, why = why it moves your bill, url = the full /guides/glossary/{slug}/ guide. Author once here; every tooltip stays in sync.

annual-maximum.def: The most your plan pays in a year — usually $1,000–$2,000.
annual-maximum.why: Once you hit it you pay 100% of everything else until the year resets, so big work is often split across two plan years.

deductible.def: What you pay yourself before the plan starts sharing costs — usually ~$50/person.
deductible.why: It's waived for preventive care, so cleanings stay free even before you've met it.

coinsurance.def: The share of a covered service the plan pays — the 100 / 80 / 50 split.
coinsurance.why: A '50% major' plan still leaves you half the crown bill, which is where the annual maximum and waiting period really bite.

waiting-period.def: How long you must hold the plan before a category becomes payable.
waiting-period.why: It's separate from when coverage 'starts' — a plan can be active yet not pay for a crown for 12 months. The #1 thing urgent-care buyers miss.

effective-date.def: The day your coverage actually begins.
effective-date.why: Different from the waiting period: coverage can begin tomorrow but still not pay for major work until the wait clears.

day-one.def: A category that's payable immediately, with no waiting period.
day-one.why: The make-or-break feature if you need a filling or crown now — only a few plans offer it for major work.

in-network.def: A dentist contracted with your plan's specific network.
in-network.why: 'Accepts' a carrier is not the same as in-network — out-of-network you can be balance-billed for the difference.

out-of-pocket.why: It's deductible + coinsurance + anything above the annual maximum — the real number to compare, not the premium alone.

allowed-amount.def: The maximum fee the plan recognizes for a service.
allowed-amount.why: Your coinsurance is figured from this, not the dentist's full charge — so 50% of the allowed amount can be less than 50% of your bill.

balance-billing.def: When an out-of-network dentist bills you the gap between their fee and the plan's allowed amount.
balance-billing.why: It's why 'in-network' matters — in-network dentists can't balance-bill you.

missing-tooth.def: Excludes coverage to replace a tooth that was already missing before your coverage began.
missing-tooth.why: It can wipe out implant or bridge coverage for the exact tooth you bought the plan for — check it before enrolling.

calendar-year.def: A benefit cycle that resets every January 1.
calendar-year.why: Your annual maximum and deductible refresh on Jan 1 — time big work around it.

plan-year.def: A benefit cycle that resets on your coverage anniversary, not January 1.
plan-year.why: If you enrolled June 15, your benefits refresh every June 15 — don't assume a January reset.

coverage-preventive.why: Usually 100% with no wait and no deductible — the part of the plan you can use immediately.

coverage-basic.why: Often 80%, sometimes after a short wait — a few plans (Guardian, MoO) cover it from day one.

coverage-major.why: Usually 50% and often gated behind a 12-month wait — the line item that decides most plan choices.

implants.why: Coverage, waits, and lifetime caps vary wildly — and the missing-tooth clause often applies.

whitening.why: Rarely covered; when it is, it's state- and tier-specific — never assume it from a marketing page.

ppo.def: A plan that lets you see any dentist but pays more when you stay in-network.
ppo.why: More flexible than an HMO/DMO, which restricts you to assigned dentists — the trade-off is a higher premium.

predetermination.def: A pre-treatment estimate from the carrier of what they'll pay.
predetermination.why: Ask for one before major work so there are no surprises — it confirms coverage, the allowed amount, and your share in writing.

cdt.def: The standardized dental procedure code (e.g., D2740 for a crown).
cdt.why: Carriers price coverage by CDT code — knowing yours lets you confirm exactly what's covered.
